caption:
7 Temporal API features that make Moment.js obsolete.

Built into JavaScript. No npm install. No bundle size. Handles time zones correctly for the first time ever.

ES2026. Shipping in all browsers.

📌 SAVE this — Moment.js is officially dead.

Which feature surprised you? 👇

#javascript #temporal #webdev #coding #programming #developer #es2026 #datetime #momentjs #codingtips

slides.title: Temporal API features that make Moment.js obsolete
slides.subtitle: Built into JavaScript. No install. Time zones that actually work.

slides.title: PlainDate — dates without time zones
slides.desc: A date that means the same thing everywhere. No midnight bugs.
slides.punchline: Finally. A date that's just a date.

slides.title: ZonedDateTime — time zones done right
slides.desc: The thing Date has been failing at for 25 years.
slides.punchline: One API. Every time zone. Correct.

slides.title: Duration — time math without bugs
slides.desc: Add 1 month and 3 days. No more manual millisecond arithmetic.

slides.title: Instant — exact moments in time
slides.desc: Like Date.now() but immutable and precise to nanoseconds.

slides.title: until() and since() — distance between dates
slides.desc: How long between two dates? One method. Any unit.

slides.title: compare() — sort dates correctly
slides.desc: Built-in comparison. No more getTime() hacks.

slides.title: from() — parse anything safely
slides.desc: ISO strings, objects, partial dates. One method handles all.

slides.title: Temporal API Cheat Sheet
slides.question: Which feature replaces the most code for you? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: Record and Tuple — immutable data in JavaScript
